# rand

```go
import "math/rand/v2"
```

Package rand implements pseudo\-random number generators suitable for tasks such as simulation, but it should not be used for security\-sensitive work.

Random numbers are generated by a [Source](<#Source>), usually wrapped in a [Rand](<#Rand>). Both types should be used by a single goroutine at a time: sharing among multiple goroutines requires some kind of synchronization.

Top\-level functions, such as [Float64](<#Float64>) and [Int](<#Int>), are safe for concurrent use by multiple goroutines.

This package's outputs might be easily predictable regardless of how it's seeded. For random numbers suitable for security\-sensitive work, see the [crypto/rand](<https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/rand/>) package.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math/rand/v2"
)

func main() {
	answers := []string{
		"It is certain",
		"It is decidedly so",
		"Without a doubt",
		"Yes definitely",
		"You may rely on it",
		"As I see it yes",
		"Most likely",
		"Outlook good",
		"Yes",
		"Signs point to yes",
		"Reply hazy try again",
		"Ask again later",
		"Better not tell you now",
		"Cannot predict now",
		"Concentrate and ask again",
		"Don't count on it",
		"My reply is no",
		"My sources say no",
		"Outlook not so good",
		"Very doubtful",
	}
	fmt.Println("Magic 8-Ball says:", answers[rand.IntN(len(answers))])
}
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Rand)</summary>
<p>

This example shows the use of each of the methods on a \*Rand. The use of the global functions is the same, without the receiver.

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math/rand/v2"
	"os"
	"text/tabwriter"
)

func main() {
	// Create and seed the generator.
	// Typically a non-fixed seed should be used, such as Uint64(), Uint64().
	// Using a fixed seed will produce the same output on every run.
	r := rand.New(rand.NewPCG(1, 2))

	// The tabwriter here helps us generate aligned output.
	w := tabwriter.NewWriter(os.Stdout, 1, 1, 1, ' ', 0)
	defer w.Flush()
	show := func(name string, v1, v2, v3 any) {
		fmt.Fprintf(w, "%s\t%v\t%v\t%v\n", name, v1, v2, v3)
	}

	// Float32 and Float64 values are in [0, 1).
	show("Float32", r.Float32(), r.Float32(), r.Float32())
	show("Float64", r.Float64(), r.Float64(), r.Float64())

	// ExpFloat64 values have an average of 1 but decay exponentially.
	show("ExpFloat64", r.ExpFloat64(), r.ExpFloat64(), r.ExpFloat64())

	// NormFloat64 values have an average of 0 and a standard deviation of 1.
	show("NormFloat64", r.NormFloat64(), r.NormFloat64(), r.NormFloat64())

	// Int32, Int64, and Uint32 generate values of the given width.
	// The Int method (not shown) is like either Int32 or Int64
	// depending on the size of 'int'.
	show("Int32", r.Int32(), r.Int32(), r.Int32())
	show("Int64", r.Int64(), r.Int64(), r.Int64())
	show("Uint32", r.Uint32(), r.Uint32(), r.Uint32())

	// IntN, Int32N, and Int64N limit their output to be < n.
	// They do so more carefully than using r.Int()%n.
	show("IntN(10)", r.IntN(10), r.IntN(10), r.IntN(10))
	show("Int32N(10)", r.Int32N(10), r.Int32N(10), r.Int32N(10))
	show("Int64N(10)", r.Int64N(10), r.Int64N(10), r.Int64N(10))

	// Perm generates a random permutation of the numbers [0, n).
	show("Perm", r.Perm(5), r.Perm(5), r.Perm(5))
}
```

#### Output

```go
Float32     0.95955694          0.8076733            0.8135684
Float64     0.4297927436037299  0.797802349388613    0.3883664855410056
ExpFloat64  0.43463410545541104 0.5513632046504593   0.7426404617374481
NormFloat64 -0.9303318111676635 -0.04750789419852852 0.22248301107582735
Int32       2020777787          260808523            851126509
Int64       5231057920893523323 4257872588489500903  158397175702351138
Uint32      314478343           1418758728           208955345
IntN(10)    6                   2                    0
Int32N(10)  3                   7                    7
Int64N(10)  8                   9                    4
Perm        [0 3 1 4 2]         [4 1 2 0 3]          [4 3 2 0 1]
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ExpFloat64"></a>

## func [ExpFloat64](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L369>)

```go
func ExpFloat64() float64
```

ExpFloat64 returns an exponentially distributed float64 in the range \(0, \+math.MaxFloat64\] with an exponential distribution whose rate parameter \(lambda\) is 1 and whose mean is 1/lambda \(1\) from the default Source. To produce a distribution with a different rate parameter, callers can adjust the output using:

```go
sample = ExpFloat64() / desiredRateParameter
```

```

func Int32N(n int32) int32
```

Int32N returns, as an int32, a pseudo\-random number in the half\-open interval \[0,n\) from the default Source. It panics if n \<= 0.

<a name="IntN"></a>

## func [IntN](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L313>)

```go
func IntN(n int) int
```

IntN returns, as an int, a pseudo\-random number in the half\-open interval \[0,n\) from the default Source. It panics if n \<= 0.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math/rand/v2"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Println(rand.IntN(100))
	fmt.Println(rand.IntN(100))
	fmt.Println(rand.IntN(100))
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="N"></a>

## func [N](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L323>)

```go
func N[Int intType](n Int) Int
```

N returns a pseudo\-random number in the half\-open interval \[0,n\) from the default Source. The type parameter Int can be any integer type. It panics if n \<= 0.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math/rand/v2"
	"time"
)

func main() {
	// Print an int64 in the half-open interval [0, 100).
	fmt.Println(rand.N(int64(100)))

	// Sleep for a random duration between 0 and 100 milliseconds.
	time.Sleep(rand.N(100 * time.Millisecond))
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="NormFloat64"></a>

## func [NormFloat64](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L360>)

```go
func NormFloat64() float64
```

NormFloat64 returns a normally distributed float64 in the range \[\-math.MaxFloat64, \+math.MaxFloat64\] with standard normal distribution \(mean = 0, stddev = 1\) from the default Source. To produce a different normal distribution, callers can adjust the output using:

```go
sample = NormFloat64() * desiredStdDev + desiredMean
```

<a name="Perm"></a>

## func [Perm](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L345>)

```go
func Perm(n int) []int
```

Perm returns, as a slice of n ints, a pseudo\-random permutation of the integers in the half\-open interval \[0,n\) from the default Source.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math/rand/v2"
)

func main() {
	for _, value := range rand.Perm(3) {
		fmt.Println(value)
	}

}
```

#### Output

```go
1
2
0
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Shuffle"></a>

## func [Shuffle](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L350>)

```go
func Shuffle(n int, swap func(i, j int))
```

Shuffle pseudo\-randomizes the order of elements using the default Source. n is the number of elements. Shuffle panics if n \< 0. swap swaps the elements with indexes i and j.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math/rand/v2"
	"strings"
)

func main() {
	words := strings.Fields("ink runs from the corners of my mouth")
	rand.Shuffle(len(words), func(i, j int) {
		words[i], words[j] = words[j], words[i]
	})
	fmt.Println(words)
}
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Slices In Unison)</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math/rand/v2"
)

func main() {
	numbers := []byte("12345")
	letters := []byte("ABCDE")
	// Shuffle numbers, swapping corresponding entries in letters at the same time.
	rand.Shuffle(len(numbers), func(i, j int) {
		numbers[i], numbers[j] = numbers[j], numbers[i]
		letters[i], letters[j] = letters[j], letters[i]
	})
	for i := range numbers {
		fmt.Printf("%c: %c\n", letters[i], numbers[i])
	}
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ChaCha8"></a>

## type [ChaCha8](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/chacha8.go#L15-L21>)

A ChaCha8 is a ChaCha8\-based cryptographically strong random number generator.

```go
type ChaCha8 struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewChaCha8"></a>

### func [NewChaCha8](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/chacha8.go#L24>)

```go
func NewChaCha8(seed [32]byte) *ChaCha8
```

NewChaCha8 returns a new ChaCha8 seeded with the given seed.

<a name="ChaCha8.AppendBinary"></a>

### func \(\*ChaCha8\) [AppendBinary](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/chacha8.go#L102>)

```go
func (c *ChaCha8) AppendBinary(b []byte) ([]byte, error)
```

AppendBinary implements the [encoding.BinaryAppender](<https://pkg.go.dev/encoding/#BinaryAppender>) interface.

<a name="ChaCha8.MarshalBinary"></a>

### func \(\*ChaCha8\) [MarshalBinary](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/chacha8.go#L112>)

```go
func (c *ChaCha8) MarshalBinary() ([]byte, error)
```

MarshalBinary implements the [encoding.BinaryMarshaler](<https://pkg.go.dev/encoding/#BinaryMarshaler>) interface.

<a name="ChaCha8.Read"></a>

### func \(\*ChaCha8\) [Read](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/chacha8.go#L54>)

```go
func (c *ChaCha8) Read(p []byte) (n int, err error)
```

Read reads exactly len\(p\) bytes into p. It always returns len\(p\) and a nil error.

If calls to Read and Uint64 are interleaved, the order in which bits are returned by the two is undefined, and Read may return bits generated before the last call to Uint64.

<a name="ChaCha8.Seed"></a>

### func \(\*ChaCha8\) [Seed](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/chacha8.go#L31>)

```go
func (c *ChaCha8) Seed(seed [32]byte)
```

Seed resets the ChaCha8 to behave the same way as NewChaCha8\(seed\).

<a name="ChaCha8.Uint64"></a>

### func \(\*ChaCha8\) [Uint64](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/chacha8.go#L38>)

```go
func (c *ChaCha8) Uint64() uint64
```

Uint64 returns a uniformly distributed random uint64 value.

<a name="PCG"></a>

## type [PCG](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/pcg.go#L18-L21>)

A PCG is a PCG generator with 128 bits of internal state. A zero PCG is equivalent to NewPCG\(0, 0\).

```go
type PCG struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

UnmarshalBinary implements the [encoding.BinaryUnmarshaler](<https://pkg.go.dev/encoding/#BinaryUnmarshaler>) interface.

<a name="Rand"></a>

## type [Rand](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L34-L36>)

A Rand is a source of random numbers.

```go
type Rand struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="New"></a>

### func [New](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L40>)

```go
func New(src Source) *Rand
```

New returns a new Rand that uses random values from src to generate other random values.

<a name="Rand.ExpFloat64"></a>

### func \(\*Rand\) [ExpFloat64](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/exp.go#L30>)

```go
func (r *Rand) ExpFloat64() float64
```

ExpFloat64 returns an exponentially distributed float64 in the range \(0, \+math.MaxFloat64\] with an exponential distribution whose rate parameter \(lambda\) is 1 and whose mean is 1/lambda \(1\). To produce a distribution with a different rate parameter, callers can adjust the output using:

```go
sample = ExpFloat64() / desiredRateParameter
```

<a name="Rand.Float32"></a>

### func \(\*Rand\) [Float32](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L214>)

```go
func (r *Rand) Float32() float32
```

Float32 returns, as a float32, a pseudo\-random number in the half\-open interval \[0.0,1.0\).

<a name="Rand.Float64"></a>

### func \(\*Rand\) [Float64](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L208>)

```go
func (r *Rand) Float64() float64
```

Float64 returns, as a float64, a pseudo\-random number in the half\-open interval \[0.0,1.0\).

<a name="Rand.Int"></a>

### func \(\*Rand\) [Int](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L57>)

```go
func (r *Rand) Int() int
```

Int returns a non\-negative pseudo\-random int.

<a name="Rand.Int32"></a>

### func \(\*Rand\) [Int32](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L54>)

```go
func (r *Rand) Int32() int32
```

Int32 returns a non\-negative pseudo\-random 31\-bit integer as an int32.

<a name="Rand.Int32N"></a>

### func \(\*Rand\) [Int32N](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L171>)

```go
func (r *Rand) Int32N(n int32) int32
```

Int32N returns, as an int32, a non\-negative pseudo\-random number in the half\-open interval \[0,n\). It panics if n \<= 0.

<a name="Rand.Int64"></a>

### func \(\*Rand\) [Int64](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L45>)

```go
func (r *Rand) Int64() int64
```

Int64 returns a non\-negative pseudo\-random 63\-bit integer as an int64.

<a name="Rand.Int64N"></a>

### func \(\*Rand\) [Int64N](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L64>)

```go
func (r *Rand) Int64N(n int64) int64
```

Int64N returns, as an int64, a non\-negative pseudo\-random number in the half\-open interval \[0,n\). It panics if n \<= 0.

<a name="Rand.IntN"></a>

### func \(\*Rand\) [IntN](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L191>)

```go
func (r *Rand) IntN(n int) int
```

IntN returns, as an int, a non\-negative pseudo\-random number in the half\-open interval \[0,n\). It panics if n \<= 0.

<a name="Rand.NormFloat64"></a>

### func \(\*Rand\) [NormFloat64](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/normal.go#L37>)

```go
func (r *Rand) NormFloat64() float64
```

NormFloat64 returns a normally distributed float64 in the range \-math.MaxFloat64 through \+math.MaxFloat64 inclusive, with standard normal distribution \(mean = 0, stddev = 1\). To produce a different normal distribution, callers can adjust the output using:

```go
sample = NormFloat64() * desiredStdDev + desiredMean
```

<a name="Rand.Perm"></a>

### func \(\*Rand\) [Perm](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L221>)

```go
func (r *Rand) Perm(n int) []int
```

Perm returns, as a slice of n ints, a pseudo\-random permutation of the integers in the half\-open interval \[0,n\).

<a name="Rand.Shuffle"></a>

### func \(\*Rand\) [Shuffle](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L233>)

```go
func (r *Rand) Shuffle(n int, swap func(i, j int))
```

Shuffle pseudo\-randomizes the order of elements. n is the number of elements. Shuffle panics if n \< 0. swap swaps the elements with indexes i and j.

<a name="Rand.Uint"></a>

### func \(\*Rand\) [Uint](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L60>)

```go
func (r *Rand) Uint() uint
```

Uint returns a pseudo\-random uint.

<a name="Rand.Uint32"></a>

### func \(\*Rand\) [Uint32](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L48>)

```go
func (r *Rand) Uint32() uint32
```

Uint32 returns a pseudo\-random 32\-bit value as a uint32.

<a name="Rand.Uint32N"></a>

### func \(\*Rand\) [Uint32N](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L180>)

```go
func (r *Rand) Uint32N(n uint32) uint32
```

Uint32N returns, as a uint32, a non\-negative pseudo\-random number in the half\-open interval \[0,n\). It panics if n == 0.

<a name="Rand.Uint64"></a>

### func \(\*Rand\) [Uint64](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L51>)

```go
func (r *Rand) Uint64() uint64
```

Uint64 returns a pseudo\-random 64\-bit value as a uint64.

<a name="Rand.Uint64N"></a>

### func \(\*Rand\) [Uint64N](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L73>)

```go
func (r *Rand) Uint64N(n uint64) uint64
```

Uint64N returns, as a uint64, a non\-negative pseudo\-random number in the half\-open interval \[0,n\). It panics if n == 0.

<a name="Rand.UintN"></a>

### func \(\*Rand\) [UintN](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L200>)

```go
func (r *Rand) UintN(n uint) uint
```

UintN returns, as a uint, a non\-negative pseudo\-random number in the half\-open interval \[0,n\). It panics if n == 0.

<a name="Source"></a>

## type [Source](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/rand.go#L29-L31>)

A Source is a source of uniformly\-distributed pseudo\-random uint64 values in the range \[0, 1\<\<64\).

A Source is not safe for concurrent use by multiple goroutines.

```go
type Source interface {
    Uint64() uint64
}
```

<a name="Zipf"></a>

## type [Zipf](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/zipf.go#L15-L25>)

A Zipf generates Zipf distributed variates.

```go
type Zipf struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewZipf"></a>

### func [NewZipf](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/zipf.go#L39>)

```go
func NewZipf(r *Rand, s float64, v float64, imax uint64) *Zipf
```

NewZipf returns a Zipf variate generator. The generator generates values k ∈ \[0, imax\] such that P\(k\) is proportional to \(v \+ k\) \*\* \(\-s\). Requirements: s \> 1 and v \>= 1.

<a name="Zipf.Uint64"></a>

### func \(\*Zipf\) [Uint64](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/math/rand/v2/zipf.go#L58>)

```go
func (z *Zipf) Uint64() uint64
```

Uint64 returns a value drawn from the Zipf distribution described by the Zipf object.
